fields.helpText: Name of blood component, reagent or service supplied to blood bank.
The abbreviation of the ITEM
If product can be modified (divided, washed, frozen, pooled, made leukocyte poor, rejuvenated, or deglycerolized) answer prompt with YES.
Identifies if entry is a blood component for transfusion, a reagent used in blood banking or a product for manufacturing use only.

Product code conforms to the uniform labeling of blood and blood  components described in either the UNIFORM LABELING OF BLOOD AND  BLOOD COMPONENTS: USERS' GUIDE SEPTEMBER 1982 (Codabar) or the  United States Industry Consensus Standard for the Uniform Labeling  of Blood and Blood Components Using ISBT 128, v 1.2.0  November 1999.
Product codes for the purpose of shipping blood component  inventory by Department of Defense (DOD) facilities.

If this product can be made from another product present in inventory enter appropriate response. If selection is D (DIVIDED) a single letter suffix will be appended (from A-E) to the unit ID depending upon the number of aliquots up to 5.  This field is used to trigger the appropriate prompts if the new product(s) will be divided units or a pool.
Either the patient and product ABO group must match or be compatible
Either the patient and product RH type must match or be compatible
If the product requires a crossmatch enter 'CROSSMATCH'  If the product requires patient plasma compatibility enter  'PLASMA/PATIENT COMPATIBILITY'
Enter the usual volume in milliliters (ml) of the component
If this product made from another one in INVENTORY enter number  of days left before the newly made component will expire.
Enter the anticoagulant mixed with the component
The maximum hours allowed between blood collection and   preparation of component.  If no entry no checking will be done.  If maximum hours exceeded component cannot be prepared.
Maximum storage time from time of donation or preparation.
If component must be further modified before release (ex. frozen RBC must  be deglycerolized before transfusion) a 'YES' is entered.
If component can be requested for a patient a 'YES' is entered.
If a patient specimen is required for the blood component the maximum  hours allowed before a new specimen is required is entered here.
If units of component must be retyped then enter 'YES'.  If units do not require retyping enter 'NO'.
If component contains mainly red blood cells enter 'YES'.
Maximum age (in days) allowed for making pediatric units

This is the pediatric component that the present component  can be made into. The screen for this field checks for three conditions: 1) Any product listed must contain the word "PEDIATRIC" in    the name. 2) Any product listed must be of the same symbology (Codabar    or ISBT128) as the current component record. 3) Any product listed must have the same anticoagulant as     the current component record.
Weight of component in grams per ml.
The maximum time in minutes from issue to completion of transfusion  of transfusion.
This field is used to determine if a component is autologous,  directed or neither type of donation.

fields.setOfCodes: 1:WHOLE BLOOD;2:RBC;3:FROZEN RBC;4:DEGLYC RBC;5:LEUCODEPLETED RBC;6:WASHED RBC;7:FFP;8:CRYO;9:RANDOM PLAT;10:APHERESIS PLAT;11:GRANULOCYTES;
fields.helpText: Used to define category for administrative data and workload.
If product is pooled enter 'YES'.  The entry is used when  running the integrity check for file 65 (Blood Inventory).
Entering 'YES' will result in prompting the user for the bag lot #  when this component is prepared from a unit in inventory.

fields.setOfCodes: 0:NO (This is a Codabar product type);1:YES (This is an ISBT128 product type);
fields.helpText: This field indicates whether the product type belongs to ISBT 128 or to Codabar.  All product types fall into two categories: Codabar and ISBT  128.  This field identifies the symbology to which each product type belongs.
These are components this product can produce.
This is a multiple field.  Contains information about all suppliers of this item.
Suggested criteria for transfusion of blood component can be listed here.
These are the tests that will determine if the request seems  reasonable based on test values when a blood component is requested.
These are instructions included in the requisition.
This field checks lab values for specified component request for  surgery.

This field points to the blood product (of the opposite symbology) that most closely matches or is equivalent to the specified blood product.   It screens on the IS ISBT field and allows only blood products of  opposite symbologies to be stored as equivalent products.  For any  ISBT 128 products, only products with IS ISBT = 0 (Codabar products) can  be selected.  The same is true for Codabar units; only blood products  with IS ISBT = 1 (ISBT 128 products) can be selected.
This field contains wkld codes that should be counted each time this blood product is issued.
